title: Catering and bar managers
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate activities of an organization or department that serves food and beverages. Directly supervise and coordinate activities of workers engaged in preparing and serving food. Prepare and cook large quantities of food for institutions, such as schools, hospitals, or cafeterias. (Based on 19 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Test cooked food by tasting and smelling to ensure palatability and flavor conformity.
Order and purchase equipment and supplies, and shop for or requisition food supplies based on estimates of future needs.
Count money, make bank deposits, and balance cash receipts and payments.
Schedule use of facilities or catering services for events, take dining reservations, and negotiate arrangements with clients.
Create specialty dishes, develop recipes, and prepare or serve menu items according to nutritional needs and costs.
Establish and enforce nutritional standards for dining establishments, and monitor menus and spending to ensure economical meal preparation.
Greets guests, escorts them to their seats, and presents them with menus and wine lists.
Monitor employee and patron activities to ensure compliance with liquor regulations and maintain a safe environment for service.
Arrange for equipment maintenance and repairs, and coordinate various services such as waste removal and pest control.
Assess staffing needs, recruit staff, and train new employees.
Record the number, type, and cost of items sold to identify unpopular or less profitable items and conduct financial activities.
Review menus and analyze recipes to assign prices and determine labor and overhead costs.
Investigate and resolve complaints regarding food quality, service, or accommodations, and monitor compliance with health and fire regulations.
Organize and direct worker training programs, evaluate employee performance, and monitor operational problems to improve service and safety.
Coordinate assignments of cooking personnel and supervise kitchen staff to ensure efficient food preparation and service.
Schedule and receive food and beverage deliveries, checking items for quality and quantity.
Maintain food and equipment inventories and keep accurate inventory records.
Monitor budgets and payroll records while ensuring expenditures are authorized and within budget.
Schedule staff hours, assign duties, and observe and evaluate workers and work procedures to ensure quality standards and service.
Monitor food preparation methods, portion sizes, and presentation to ensure proper standards.
Keep records as required by government agencies regarding sanitation or food subsidies.
Establish performance standards for staff and customer service.
Estimate food and beverage consumption to anticipate purchase needs.
Perform various food preparation or service tasks, including cooking and serving food, especially during busy periods.
Create drink recipes, plan bar menus, and direct operations of a cocktail lounge or bar.
Bake breads, rolls, and pastries, and prepare items for serving.
Perform food preparation tasks such as cleaning, cutting, and cooking vegetables and meats, and baking breads and pastries.
Cook foodstuffs according to menus or special dietary needs, and serve prepared food to patrons.
Clean and inspect kitchen areas and equipment to ensure sanitary food preparation and service practices.
Serve wine, beer, and other beverages as requested and ensure bar cleanliness.
Stock and organize kitchens, bars, and dining areas, ensuring all supplies are available and stored properly.
Plan and prepare food for various events, including parties and special functions.
Monitor food supplies' freshness and store items safely at correct temperatures.
Prepare and serve cold drinks and desserts, using appropriate machines and equipment.
Participate in cleaning activities in kitchens and dining areas, including managing service areas.
Deliver orders to kitchens, pick up, and serve food when ready.
Take customer orders and provide menu details or suggest products to customers.
Monitor and record food temperatures to ensure safety and quality.
Compile and maintain records of food use and expenditures.
Prepare appetizers and garnishes for various dishes.
Discuss menu items with supervisory staff and plan according to special event needs and costs.
Perform personnel activities and conduct assessments to ensure quality staffing.
Wash dishes, utensils, and bar equipment after use.
Plan, prepare, and deliver meals to individuals with special dietary needs, and specify food portions and courses, production and time sequences, and workstation and equipment arrangements.
Request and record customer orders, compute bills, and accept payments using cash registers or other means, and communicate with customers regarding orders, comments, and complaints.
Perform cleaning duties including cleaning and sanitizing work areas, utensils, equipment, and facilities to maintain hygiene and safety standards.
Inspect dining and serving areas, restrooms, and kitchens to ensure cleanliness and proper setup, and manage inventories of food, equipment, and supplies, reporting shortages as needed.
Supervise and coordinate activities of dining room staff to ensure prompt and courteous service, including serving food, managing guest seating, and resolving customer complaints.
Train workers in food preparation, service, sanitation, and safety procedures, and develop and coordinate educational programs for staff.
Greet customers, provide menus, and assist in serving beverages and food as needed, ensuring guests are satisfied with their dining experience.
Develop departmental objectives, budgets, policies, procedures, and strategies, including overseeing the purchase, planning, and upkeep of facilities and supplies.
Hold regular meetings with staff to discuss operational issues and ensure compliance with service standards, and document progress on goals.
Organize and promote interest in recreational activities for guests, and direct special activities or events as needed.
Provide staff with assistance in performing difficult or complicated duties, and train workers in proper operational procedures and functions while explaining company policies.
Apply customer feedback to service improvement efforts and resolve customer complaints regarding worker performance or services rendered.
Inspect work areas or operating equipment to ensure conformance to established standards in areas such as cleanliness or maintenance.
Recruit, hire staff members, and assign work schedules to ensure quality and timely delivery of service.
Take disciplinary action to address performance problems and recommend and implement measures to improve worker motivation, work methods, or customer services.
Meet with managers or other supervisors to stay informed of changes affecting operations and workers.
Observe and evaluate workers' appearance and performance to ensure quality service and compliance with specifications.
Furnish customers with information on events or activities.
Direct marketing, advertising, or other customer recruitment efforts.
Participate in continuing education to stay abreast of industry trends and developments.
Examine and demonstrate work tasks to new workers or assign training tasks to experienced workers.
Enforce safety rules and regulations.
